reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to propose a remediation plan: backup /etc/shadow, check ownership root:shadow, set correct ownership if needed, set permissions to 0000 (i.e., remove all access bits) as per recommendation. Also ensure SELinux context is correct (restore with restorecon). Use read_file before modify? It's a binary file; perhaps we can just read to verify? But rule says always use read_file before modifying config file; okay we will read /etc/shadow to inspect (maybe not needed but to satisfy rule). Then backup via cp. Then chown root:shadow if needed. Then chmod 0000. Then restore SELinux context. Maybe also verify mode after change via stat.

Plan must be described then call review_plan.
